messages.content: how's jordan
Came Saturday. Sat at the table for the first time. Eight months in and he picked the table.
jordan brought new pages?
Yeah. The grandmother poem has a second half now. He's letting it breathe.
the quiet kid
Jordan. Spoke first this week. Asked Iris what she thought of his line.
jordan still coming
Every Saturday. That's the only metric I'm tracking with him.
the writing teen
Iris. Still writing thrillers. NYU rec is in. We hear in March.
iris share anything new
Read a paragraph aloud last week. First time in two years. The room held.
how's iris
Tired in a senior-year way. Wrote a piece about her grandmother's house being sold. She's letting me read it.
the kid who writes too much
Cole. I'm starting to tell him this fall. The pages are getting too dense for the work he's doing.
how's cole
He noticed himself this week. Said the third page didn't deserve to be there. Big moment.
new kid coming back
Mac. Three Saturdays now. Sat at the table this week.
mac doing okay
Yeah. Showed me a notebook page on the way out. First show-me of theirs.
the snack kid
Reed. Asked his first question two weeks ago. Now he asks one each Saturday.
reed showing up
Yeah. Still mostly for snacks. The reason doesn't matter. The showing up does.
the painter
Alma. Painted three hours straight on Saturday. She doesn't show me what.
alma sharing yet
Not yet. She works face to the wall. I respect the wall.
who else is in the room saturdays
Jordan, Iris, Cole, Mac, Reed, Alma, Devon's brother. Maybe seven on a good Saturday.
the new transfer
Sixteen. From the high school across town. Quiet. Hasn't picked a tool yet.
any new kids
One. From the program at McCallum. Diego sent her over. Hasn't been to a Saturday yet.
jordan share his thing
Not yet. He keeps the folder. The folder is the thing.
iris going to NYU
If they take her. Rec is sent. We hear March.
cole's pages this week
Tighter. He's been listening when I asked which sentence was carrying weight.
mac picked a tool
Notebook and pencil. Won't draft on a screen. That's information.
reed talking more
A little. He'll comment on someone else's work before he'll show his own. I'm letting it stay that way.
how was saturday with jordan
He laughed. First time I've heard it. Cole said something stupid.
iris's piece
It's good. The grandmother part is doing the work. The middle wanders. I'll tell her tuesday.
any difficult kids this week
One. Stormed out at noon. Came back at one fifteen and sat in the corner. That's a good ratio.
the difficult kid
Came back. Asked Aaron a question on the way out. Aaron answered. That's the loop.
how was the workshop
Six kids. Three were on. Three were off. The on three carried the off three. It works.
who's running out of pages
Cole. He's filling notebooks faster than I can recommend the next one.
who's the new face
A kid named Wren. Fourteen. Brought by her older sister. She'll be back.
new student writer
Hasn't said her name yet. I'm not going to ask. She'll give it when she gives it.
any kids leaving
One graduated out. Iris will be next if NYU takes her. The room is always recomposing.
any teen drama this week
Two of them like the same boy at school. They're not friends to begin with so it's quieter than it sounds.
who surprised you this week
Reed. He wrote a poem. Eight lines. Showed it to me on the way out.
any hard moments
Jordan's mom called Friday. Asked about him. I gave her the version that was true and not the version that was hers to ask for.
what's eating you today
Mid-City Foundation reapplication. Two weeks out. Narrative section won't sit right.
grant news
Texas Commission rejected. Reapplying spring. Houston Endowment still pending.
how many grants this fall
Six. Mid-City, Texas Commission, two private donors, Houston Endowment, the Hatch Foundation. Two are renewals.
any wins
The Hatch came in. Twelve thousand. Funded the Saturday snack budget for a year.
what's the headache
The Mid-City budget worksheet. Aaron's notes don't match Camila's reconciliation. Three hours of math today.
deadline today
Mid-City. Submitted at four. Fifteen minutes early.
good news
The Wren Family Trust came through. Twenty-five hundred. Small but unrestricted.
any reapplications
Texas Commission spring. Hatch quarterly. The pattern is steady.
what fell through
The corporate sponsor. They pivoted to STEM. We're not STEM. They were never going to fund us long-term.
narrative section killing you
Yeah. They want story. They reject for tone the rest of the year. The genre is not consistent.
funding outlook
Through June we're funded. After that, two big grants pending. If both come, fine. If neither, harder year.
any funder feedback
One reviewer wrote 'lacks scalability vision.' We're a teen writing program in one building. The scalability is the limit.
who you waiting on
Mid-City. Eight weeks out. Then Houston Endowment. Then maybe the city arts grant if it's funded this round.
any new sources
A foundation Maya knows from her chapbook days. We pitch in March.
how's the writing of the grants
Like writing fiction in a register I'd never read. Effective enough.
what's the next deadline
Houston Endowment in three weeks. Texas Commission in six. Then quiet until April.
got a no today
Yeah. The smaller one. Hurt for an hour. Moved on.
running thin
Not yet. Through June. After that depends on Mid-City.
how's maya
Tired this week. Asked the right hard question on Tuesday. Made me rewrite a paragraph that needed it.
your boss happy
Maya doesn't do happy. She does satisfied. The grant draft satisfied her. That's the bar.
talk to maya today
Yeah. Three minutes at her door about the budget reconciliation. She sees the problem. She'll handle it.
the boss
Maya. Wore the FUND THE ARTS mug today. That means the funder call went badly.
how's your boss dynamic
Maya doesn't coddle. I've gotten better feedback from her in three years than from anyone in any classroom.
maya say anything
Said the rewrite was 'closer.' That's a compliment from her. I'll take it.
how's it with the boss
Maya's protecting the budget hard this quarter. There's something she's not telling me yet.
your boss got back to you
Yeah. Three-line email. Approved. Move on. That's how Maya communicates.
maya in
Yeah. Door open. That means anyone can come in. That's how she runs the office.
talk to your boss about the kid
Maya. Yeah. Five minutes. She agreed with the call. I was second-guessing myself.
how's the boss this week
Quieter than usual. Something's on her mind. I'm not asking yet.
any feedback
Maya stopped at my door for thirty seconds. Said the budget worksheet was 'fine.' That's her highest praise. I'll take it.
any conflict at work
Maya and I disagreed on a kid. She backed me. I expected her to push back and she didn't. That's its own information.
who else weighs in on programming
Maya, Aaron, sometimes Camila on budget questions. Lou for the workshop arc.
what's the boss reading
Saw a Maggie Nelson on her desk. We've never talked about it. I won't bring it up.
how's the boss about the grant
Maya signed off. Asked me one question I hadn't thought about. The question made the draft better.
feeling supported by your boss
Maya. Yeah. The way she shows up isn't warm. But she shows up.
how was the call with the funder
Maya took it. Came out smiling. That's rare. Means it went better than expected.
how's aaron
Steady. Sara's mother is sick. He didn't say it but his shoulders told me.
who carries you at work
Aaron. Methodical where I'm fast. He fact-checks my optimism.
aaron in today
Yeah. Brown messenger bag on the chair. We split the back office until three.
the steady coworker
Aaron. Sat with a kid for forty minutes saturday until the kid said the thing. That's how he works.
aaron say anything
He told me to slow down on the budget worksheet. He was right. I was racing.
how's the coworker
Aaron's wife had a hard week. He's been carrying it without saying it. I'm watching.
who's on saturday with you
Aaron. He sets up the tables. I run the kid intake. We've never had to talk about who does what.
any news from aaron
His daughter is into birds. He brought a field guide to lunch and read for forty minutes. Good day for him.
the methodical coworker
Aaron. Caught a budget error I missed. He's the reason the books balance.
aaron solid this week
Yeah. He always is. The unit of his is one steady week.
good colleague today
Aaron talked Cole down on Saturday after the storm-out. I would have escalated. He didn't. Cole came back.
who's holding it together
Aaron. As usual. He doesn't get credit for it because he doesn't ask for any.
who's at the office today
Aaron, me, Camila. Maya's at a board meeting until three.
who's running the back today
Aaron and me, taking turns. He's at the desk this morning. I'm there after lunch.
who's that older guy at the studio
Lou. Retired English teacher. Runs Tuesday workshop. Eleven years unpaid.
lou running tonight
Yeah. Six adults, one teen. He'll do the prosody piece they always ask for and he always pretends not to want to do.
best advice you ever got at work
Lou. Write to the line, not at it. Don't lose that. I haven't.
lou wear the denim today
Yeah. Tuesday. Always.
lou say anything good lately
He told me to read Jane Hirshfield. I had been meaning to. He's always two months ahead of where I'm meaning to.
how's lou
Slower this winter. Sat through the workshop instead of standing. He'll be back to standing in spring.
who runs the tuesday session
Lou. Same chair. Same denim shirt rotation. Same half-grin when someone reads something true.
any advice from lou
He told me to stop fixing the second draft of the grant. Said I was past the point of help.
office tradition
Camila brings tamales the first week of December. Has done it nine years. We don't make a thing of it. We just eat them.
who handles the books
Camila. Tuesdays and Thursdays. She maintains a spreadsheet system I have not attempted to learn.
camila in
Yeah. Tuesday. Came in with a bag of pan dulce from the bakery on Riverside.
anyone call you anything
Camila calls me mija. The first time it felt like overshooting. Now I look forward to it.
camila got news
Her grandson is starting kindergarten. She showed me a picture of him in his uniform. She's never shown me a picture before.
who's the bookkeeper
Camila. Sixty-one. Knows everyone's business. Tells almost none of it.
camila say anything today
She caught a coding error in the Q2 books. Said it gently. The number was off by twelve dollars and twelve cents.
who's the longest-serving
Camila and Lou. Nine and eleven years. They're the substrate.
good saturday
Yeah. The room hit the right noise around two. Sufjan low. Kids working. I just kept refilling the snacks.
saturday went how
Rained at noon. Kids stayed inside. Quieter than usual. Iris read a draft, which she has never done.
saturday wrap
Tables broken down by 4:30. Snack count at the end was one apple short. Someone took it home. Aaron noticed. We laughed.
open studio today
Yeah. Loud start, quieter middle, loud finish. The shape of a good one.
how was the saturday
Mac stayed an extra twenty minutes. That's three Saturdays running.
weekend at the studio
Eight kids. Three new pages from Cole. Iris finally let me read the thriller draft. The thriller is good.
how's the room saturday
Calm. The kids are mature this group. Less drama than fall.
the studio noise level
Loud. Devon's brother brought a friend. The friend wrote three pages. Big day.
saturday again
Yeah. Always. The schedule is weather. It just happens.
how was the open studio
Six kids. Aaron set up the new long table. The room felt different. We'll keep it.
saturday traffic
Strong. Eleven kids. Most we've had since fall. Aaron and I had to break out the second snack table.
the open studio go well
Yeah. Margarita's piano in the corner got played for the first time in months. One of the kids tried it.
saturday afternoon update
Cole and Mac in the same corner for an hour. They were not collaborating. They were near each other and both writing. That counts.
how's the saturday model holding
It works. The unscheduled afternoon is the structure. Removing more would make it worse.
that one moment from saturday
Jordan asked Iris if she'd read his line. She said yes. They sat at the same table for twenty minutes. That moment.
how was the room when you closed up
Quiet. The light through the high windows was gold. I stayed five minutes after the kids left. Aaron pretended not to notice.
what's the headache today
AC. Studio at sixty-three, back office at eighty. Repair guy comes Thursday.
office headache
Coffee machine finally died. Camila ordered a new one. Three days without.
what's broken today
Front door lock. Maintenance three weeks out. We're using the side door.
any operational fires
Yeah. Volunteer poet group double-booked Tuesday with Lou. Sorted but ate forty minutes.
the schedule conflict
Saturday open studio at the same time as the parent and tot session. Camila spotted it. Moved parent and tot to Sunday.
what's the budget thing
Q2 numbers off by twelve dollars and twelve cents. Found this morning. Camila will reconcile by Thursday.
any building issues
Roof leak in the back office corner. Buckets out. Landlord coming next week. We've been here before.
what's draining you
The expense reports for the trip. Six receipts. Three missing. I'll write to the venue tomorrow.
insurance update
Premium up nine percent. We knew it was coming. Camila built the cushion in December.
what went sideways today
The supply order. Wrong sketchpad type. The kids want the right one back. Returned them. Reordering tomorrow.
power out
For an hour this morning. Nothing was lost. Aaron rebooted the printer. We laughed at it.
the AC again
Yeah. Repair guy says compressor next time. We're dancing with that money.
what's the small thing eating you
The bathroom door doesn't latch. It's been three months. I'll fix it myself this weekend.
